scenes.name: 40% RGD Rule Could Drive Black Market

elements.name: Caption 1
elements.content: Now it's going to be quite an experiment because 40% RGD, up to 25% sports betting next year. Just as a prominent executive in the industry, how do you reflect on what is quite a seismic shift for us? Well, I think the UK has always been in the forefront when it comes to regulation and how to regulate the industry. However, right now, when you look at the numbers from the UK, the fastest growing part seems to be the black market, which is not a good thing for the industry, it's not a good thing for the UK and so forth.

ad26f993-3655-4bf4-9739-bb7124acb08a:whi.text: Hello viewers, Tim Bull here on the huddle and I'm with Jesper Svensson, the executive of the year for the Global Gaming Awards EMEA. Jesper, thanks for joining us. Wow, yes, thank you very much. So you've been on the huddle plenty of times before, but you've never been on the huddle and no one's ever been on the huddle as a two-time winner of the executive of the year of the EMEA Awards. You know, we caught up a little bit after the show yesterday, after the event, but how do you reflect, you know, now maybe you've had a bit of thinking time on the achievement? Listen, it's such an honor because being the... Voted by Piers, i think it's the best price you can get so i'm i'm super happy very humble uh over it um however very glad you know i'm gonna i'm gonna put you on the spot a little bit because yeah because because you've won more than once it's not a fluke you know you're doing something right whether it's your your clients or your partners or your team people like you as i've said before we got a comment on youtube that you're the jürgen klopp of i gaming so you know you've obviously there's something you're doing right if you had if you had some advice for kind of young executives coming through you know what is it that you do or what is it that you think they should do what are some kind of guiding principles well i don't know when you enjoy what you're doing it's so much easier and it's work but put your heart into it you know i mean we all know we spend too many hours of our lives working but if we enjoy it it's more fun you know and i think it's noticeable for the people who who enjoy it then you put a bit of heart into it that's that's the way to go yeah i mean i would call heart to the agree i think yeah um because you're a liverpool fan and you know what it means with heart yeah absolutely um well i'm sure we'll talk about liverpool a little bit later on um but i wanted to ask about bets and bets and also won an award as an operator social responsibility of the year and this i've said it before it's an important category but it's easy to talk about social responsibility but by you know by winning at the global gaming awards and as you see your peers voting for you it shows that you're not just talking you're doing no so what is it that bets and is doing that really shows your commitment well i mean it's something we always speak about we keep it in the center of what we do as well because we acknowledge that our industry has a very very beautiful society the positive side, but also there is is, like with any industry like this, there is also a problematic side, you can say. And when you acknowledge that and you work with that, you can take it a long way. And I'm really pleased that the industry noticed all the good work we do to protect our players, basically. Betsson, famously Latam. Yeah. So, you know, kind of you were Latam before it was cool type thing, you know. I wanted to ask your views on it because you've got the Brazil license as well. I wanted to ask your thoughts on, because covering that market from our perspective has been a real rollercoaster this last year. You've had court hearings about influencers where an influencer took a selfie with a senator. We'll never forget that image. You know, you've got potential tax rises. But it's also been the first 12 months of regulated gaming, so a lot of positives. What's your thoughts just on Brazil? Just a little general on this kind of rollercoaster we're going on. I mean, massive market. And over time, it it will be a good market not just for the industry but for for us as well we have been cautious up until this point but we gradually now start to invest more and more into it but remember it's we always say it's a marathon not the sprint you know so we give it some time and i hope in three to five years we will be one of the important players in that one today we're not but we are aiming to get there yeah absolutely the marathon not a sprint um ideology is something we kind of heard in in the us as well because the way the certain states looked a few years ago in the us very different now a lot of those players who might have led at the start might not even be in the market at the moment so um as you say um looking from afar or you know not necessarily too too far i also just wanted your thoughts generally on on on the uk um because for years you know the uk was was was looked at as a mature market, as an example. Now it's going to be quite an experiment because 40% RGD, up to 25% sports betting next year. Just as a prominent executive in the industry, how do you reflect on what is quite a seismic shift for us? Well, I think the UK has always been at the forefront when it comes to regulation and how to regulate the industry. However, right now, when you look at the numbers from the UK, the fastest growing part seems to be the black market, which is not a good thing for the industry, it's not a good thing for the UK and so forth. I think you need to strike the balance because in the end it's about the player. What does the player do? And if you limit the experience experience too much you will see a big black market economy and i hope the balance of that will will swing back in a way where the players will choose to play with the regulated companies in the uk paying taxes following the rules but right now it seems that there is a lot of players going elsewhere you know and that's not a good thing so something has to change over time i'm glad you mentioned the black market because every conference every every interview and not just about me in general we talk about the black market we talk about kind of the the impact um in terms of practical ways of addressing it whether it's regulators operators um because we talk about the threat of it a lot what apart from you know regulation and unfavorable tax rates and um and a better player experience how can we how can we stop kind of illegal sites well um as an end user as a gambler or a player you don't care about taxes i mean that's for companies right so you want to have the best experience possible product wise and overall wise so that's where the competition should in many ways be and um here is where um it's such a fine balance of how can you have a good experience without restricting the player too much in the sense of you know yourself when you when a company asks you for show me your pace like show me everything like this if you trust it it's okay and you feel like okay this company may protect me or not but many people also go i'm i'm not going to show that to any company right so it's it's a balance it's a balance thing and you need to get that uh get that right basically yeah i mean sensing even from your answer that it's not it's not easy to get that balance there's no one particular solution no no it isn't but if you work on the product and try to make the best online experience you can have then you will ultimately win that race so to say though and we all want a sustainable sustainable industry and in a sustainable market a final area for me uh a little bit more positive i want to talk about sports betting and sports in general uh i feel like every time we sit down for one of these there's a major tournament in the summer or a world cup and there is a world cup this year um obviously there's there's an american angle to it in terms of time zones and and uh you know where it's being hosted but as an operator operator uh you know anything you're doing different this year what are the expectations i'm i'm gonna guess the first thing you'll say is it's going to be the biggest tournament because it's always the thing and for us also latin america is our biggest sports betting market right so for us the time zone is perfect for that and i think it's going to be fantastic i can't wait and uh who will win what do you think well that was gonna be my next question um i think did you did you predict argentina last time yeah i ran you i think you did it right here i'm gonna say and i might be i might be biased because i really enjoy covering these markets but i'm gonna say argentina brazil or england but i do have people in my team who are very confident about spain after the euros as well yeah so i think it the english always think england will win yes if you ask i'm aware of what you're referring to if you ask my team in argentina they are not putting england up there but i would say france this time okay okay yeah yeah come back i mean it's time interesting um i'd say england have the squad but of course whether the mentality that's often where it goes wrong um yeah but it's yeah too few skills in english well yeah there's one playing in spain at the moment yes yes um and okay who's going to win the champions league in the premier league as well it might be a sore topic for us at the moment but yeah it's hard i still believe i um i think liverpool win the champions league and um isaac comes back and score the final goal you heard it here first um but let's say let's say a plan b if that doesn't quite happen who would win it if if not for the obviously the guaranteed favorites liverpool i think barcelona is okay they're looking they're looking sharp interesting I think Real Madrid should hire Zidane for six months get him back in and then they'll win the Champions League I don't think they will but that's what they should do yeah so let's hope they don't do that the Premier League I don't know maybe we just don't discuss the Premier League it's up to you do you think Arsenal can finally hold on oh dear taking our Liverpool hats off ever so slightly yeah well City you can't you can't roll them out you know I'd like to I'd like to hope so yeah the price on Arsenal is too low for me to put any money on it if I have to put something down today I would put it on City well the price on Liverpool is pretty good but you wouldn't put any money on us unfortunately this year I will have a cheeky much that's it well Jasper thanks very much great to talk football congratulations again on winning Executive of the Year for the second time thank you and thank you for your time today thank you so much thank you .

candidates.transcriptSnippet: Now it's going to be quite an experiment because 40% RGD, up to 25% sports betting next year. Just as a prominent executive in the industry, how do you reflect on what is quite a seismic shift for us? Well, I think the UK has always been at the forefront when it comes to regulation and how to regulate the industry. However, right now, when you look at the numbers from the UK, the fastest growing part seems to be the black market, which is not a good thing for the industry, it's not a good thing for the UK and so forth.
candidates.title: 40% RGD Rule Could Drive Black Market
candidates.rationale: Strong numeric hook (40% RGD, 25% sports tax) that surprises viewers; payoff warns of black-market growth and urges balance—clear stakes, concrete numbers, high viral potential.
